channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026 is a transformative career development initiative designed to support young African graduates in their transition from education into meaningful employment, entrepreneurship, and leadership roles. This highly impactful program offers 12-month paid internship placements across Africa, connecting talented youth with organizations that are shaping the future of the continent. Launched […]

The post Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026 is a transformative career development initiative designed to support young African graduates in their transition from education into meaningful employment, entrepreneurship, and leadership roles. This highly impactful program offers 12-month paid internship placements across Africa, connecting talented youth with organizations that are shaping the future of the continent.

Launched to address the growing need for structured career pathways for young people, the program equips participants with hands-on work experience, leadership development, mentorship, and professional networking opportunities. Since its inception in 2023, the program has successfully supported over 1,000 young professionals across 22 African countries, including young women, persons with disabilities, refugees, and internally displaced persons.

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program continues to expand its reach, offering ongoing application opportunities for eligible candidates across different regions in Africa.

About the Mastercard Foundation Associates Program

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is a flagship initiative under the Mastercard Foundation’s broader mission to enable young people in Africa to access dignified and fulfilling work opportunities.

The program is built on strategic partnerships with:

  • Leading employers

  • Development organizations

  • Recruitment platforms

  • Foundation partner institutions

Through these partnerships, the program provides structured internship placements that help young professionals gain relevant work experience and develop critical career skills.

The Associates Program focuses on:

  • Bridging the gap between education and employment

  • Supporting youth employability and entrepreneurship

  • Building leadership capacity among young Africans

  • Promoting inclusive participation across diverse groups

Program Focus Areas

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program prioritizes inclusivity and impact across several thematic areas.

1. Disability Inclusion

The program ensures equal opportunities for young people with disabilities, providing accessible pathways into employment and professional development.

Read More

2. Education and Career Transitions

The initiative supports graduates in transitioning from academic life into professional careers or entrepreneurship, addressing the gap between education systems and labor market demands.

Read More

3. Refugees and Displaced Persons

The program actively includes refugees and internally displaced youth, offering them opportunities to rebuild their careers and livelihoods through structured internships.

Read More

Program Structure and Journey

The Associates Program follows a structured journey that supports participants from application to career transition.

1. Recruitment and Selection

Applicants are selected based on eligibility criteria, skills, and potential to benefit from the program.

2. Internship Placement

Successful candidates are placed in 12-month internship roles with partner organizations across Africa or in remote positions.

3. Transition Pathways

After completing the internship, participants receive support to transition into:

  • Full-time employment

  • Entrepreneurship opportunities

  • Advanced career pathways

What Participants Gain

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program offers a comprehensive package of benefits designed to prepare participants for long-term career success.

1. 12-Month Internship Placement

Participants are placed in structured internship roles that provide real-world professional experience.

2. Hands-On Work Experience

Associates gain practical exposure by working on real projects within organizations, enhancing their technical and professional competencies.

3. Leadership, Employability, and Entrepreneurship Skills

The program includes training and development opportunities that focus on:

  • Leadership development

  • Workplace readiness

  • Entrepreneurial thinking

  • Problem-solving and innovation

4. Mentorship and Professional Networks

Participants receive guidance from experienced professionals and gain access to valuable networks that support career growth.

5. Monthly Stipend

Associates receive a stipend to support them financially throughout the duration of the internship.

6. Career Transition Support

The program provides structured support to help participants move into employment or entrepreneurship after completing their internships.

Why You Should Apply

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is one of the most impactful early-career opportunities available to young Africans today.

It offers:

  • A direct pathway from education to employment

  • Exposure to professional work environments

  • Access to global networks and mentors

  • Practical skills for long-term career success

  • Opportunities to contribute to meaningful work across Africa

For young graduates seeking to build strong careers, this program provides a powerful foundation for professional growth and impact.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to a wide range of young people across Africa who are seeking early-career opportunities.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  1. Be aged between 18 and 35 years

  2. Be a recent graduate with 0–2 years of work experience

  3. Be a Mastercard Foundation Scholars Program Alumni or alumni of Mastercard Foundation programs (preferred but not always mandatory depending on roles)

  4. Be a young person residing in Africa

  5. Be seeking meaningful early-career experience

Commitment to Inclusion

The Mastercard Foundation Associates Program is intentionally inclusive and aims to ensure representation from:

  • Young women

  • Persons with disabilities

  • Refugees and internally displaced persons

  • Youth from underserved and marginalized communities

This inclusive approach ensures that the program contributes to equitable access to opportunities across Africa.

Where and How to Apply

Applicants must apply based on their country of residence or eligible remote roles. Applications are open on a rolling basis, depending on available opportunities in each region.

Applicants can access tips and guidance joining the Mastercard Foundation Transitions Community on LinkedIn.

Application Platforms by Region

1. South Africa

  • Apply through the Youth Employment Service (YES)

2. Anglophone West Africa

  • Apply through Jobberman

3. Francophone Africa

  • Apply through Talent2Africa

4. East Africa

  • Apply through Shortlist Futures

5. Remote Opportunities

  • Apply through Talentum

6. Southern Africa

  • Applications expected to open soon

Applicants are advised to monitor these platforms regularly, as roles are posted continuously.

Additional Resources for Applicants

Interested candidates can join the Mastercard Foundation Transitions Community on LinkedIn, where they can access:

  • Application tips

  • Career guidance

  • Program updates

  • Networking opportunities

Application Deadline

There is no fixed deadline, as applications are accepted on a rolling basis depending on available internship roles.

Applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible when roles are posted.

Read the frequently asked questions
Learn HERE for more information about the Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026.
To discover more global Internship opportunities, visit OFY HERE.

The post Mastercard Foundation Associates Program 2026: 12-Month Paid Internship Opportunity for Young African Graduates Across 22+ Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Global Youth Biodiversity Network (GYBN) Fellowship Program 2026 is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for young changemakers worldwide to engage in biodiversity conservation, youth advocacy, storytelling, policy engagement, and community development. Since its inception in 2022, the GYBN Fellowship Program has served as a powerful platform for youth-led action—supporting young leaders to design […]

The post GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Global Youth Biodiversity Network (GYBN) Fellowship Program 2026 is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for young changemakers worldwide to engage in biodiversity conservation, youth advocacy, storytelling, policy engagement, and community development.

Since its inception in 2022, the GYBN Fellowship Program has served as a powerful platform for youth-led action—supporting young leaders to design and implement impactful projects in their communities while connecting them to global environmental movements such as COP15 and COP16.

This year, multiple fellowship tracks are available, each designed to empower youth with skills, networks, and real-world experience to drive environmental and social change.

About the GYBN Fellowship Program

The GYBN Fellowship Program is a global initiative aimed at:

  • Supporting youth-led environmental action
  • Strengthening leadership capacity among young people
  • Promoting biodiversity conservation and sustainability
  • Amplifying youth voices in global policy spaces

Through its previous fellowship cycle (2024–2025), the program:

  • Supported 14 GYBN Chapters globally
  • Delivered 77+ community-based activities
  • Impacted 267+ communities, schools, and institutions

These initiatives included:

  • Landscape restoration
  • Environmental education programs
  • Youth mobilization campaigns
  • Indigenous and local community engagement
  • Creative activism and storytelling

Available GYBN Fellowships 2026

Applicants can apply to one of the following four fellowship tracks:

1. Youth Communications & Storytelling Fellowship

  • Focuses on amplifying youth voices and narratives
  • Supports creative storytelling, media, and communication strategies
  • Strengthens global youth engagement through impactful content creation

APPLY HERE

2. Youth Policy Support Fellowship

  • Engages fellows in biodiversity policy processes
  • Supports youth participation in regional and global advocacy spaces
  • Provides hands-on experience in policy coordination and systems change

APPLY HERE

3. Youth Community Building Fellowship

  • Focuses on strengthening grassroots youth networks
  • Encourages collaboration across cultures and regions
  • Builds solidarity and collective action within the GYBN network

APPLY HERE

4. Youth Capacity Building Fellowship

  • Develops learning spaces for youth leadership
  • Encourages peer-to-peer learning and knowledge sharing
  • Focuses on long-term leadership development in biodiversity conservation

APPLY HERE

Key Benefits of the GYBN Fellowship 2026

Participants in the program will gain:

  • Access to a global network of youth leaders
  • Opportunities to engage in international environmental platforms (e.g., COP events)
  • Hands-on experience in leadership, advocacy, and project implementation
  • Capacity-building through mentorship and collaborative learning
  • Exposure to global biodiversity and sustainability initiatives
  • Opportunity to lead impactful, community-based environmental projects

Eligibility Criteria

To apply for the GYBN Fellowship 2026, applicants must:

  • Be young individuals passionate about biodiversity and environmental action
  • Demonstrate leadership potential and commitment to community impact
  • Be actively engaged or interested in environmental, social, or youth-led initiatives
  • Be willing to collaborate in a global network
  • Have the ability to contribute to one of the fellowship tracks

Application Timeline

  • Application Deadline: 7 April 2026

Applicants are encouraged to apply early and ensure all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

Why You Should Apply

The GYBN Fellowship is more than just a program—it is a movement that empowers youth to:

  • Lead real environmental change in their communities
  • Gain international exposure and recognition
  • Build lifelong networks with global changemakers
  • Develop critical skills in leadership, policy, communication, and sustainability

This fellowship provides a unique opportunity to transform your passion for biodiversity into meaningful, measurable impact.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Select their preferred fellowship track
  2. Prepare all required application materials
  3. Submit their application through the official platform before the deadline

Click HERE to learn more about the GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026.

The post GYBN Youth Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Global Opportunities for Young Leaders in Biodiversity, Policy, Communication & Community Building appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Fully Funded Graduate Assistantships at the University of Tennessee 2026
item.dc:creator: Ankita Baidya
item.description:
Graduate assistantships at the University of Tennessee represent one of the most competitive and rewarding funding opportunities for postgraduate students seeking to study in the United States. These assistantships are designed to support students financially while equipping them with valuable academic, teaching, research, and administrative experience. Unlike traditional scholarships, graduate assistantships combine financial support with […]

The post Fully Funded Graduate Assistantships at the University of Tennessee 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Graduate assistantships at the University of Tennessee represent one of the most competitive and rewarding funding opportunities for postgraduate students seeking to study in the United States. These assistantships are designed to support students financially while equipping them with valuable academic, teaching, research, and administrative experience.

Unlike traditional scholarships, graduate assistantships combine financial support with hands-on professional development, making them highly attractive to both domestic and international students.

What is a Graduate Assistantship?

A graduate assistantship is a structured funding arrangement where a student works part-time for the university while pursuing a graduate degree. These roles are typically divided into three main categories:

1. Teaching Assistantships (TA)

Students assist faculty with teaching responsibilities such as:

  • Leading tutorials or lab sessions
  • Grading assignments
  • Supporting classroom instruction

2. Research Assistantships (RA)

Students contribute to faculty-led research projects:

  • Data collection and analysis
  • Academic publishing support
  • Laboratory or field research

3. Administrative/Professional Assistantships

Students work in university departments:

  • Office support
  • Program coordination
  • Student services

Graduate assistantships generally require 10–20 hours of work per week, depending on whether the role is part-time or half-time.

Benefits of the University of Tennessee Graduate Assistantships

Graduate assistantships at the University of Tennessee provide a comprehensive financial and professional package, including:

1. Monthly Stipend – Students receive a salary to cover living expenses. The amount varies by department and workload.

2. Tuition Waiver (Full or Partial) – Many assistantships cover a significant portion—or all—of tuition costs.

3. Professional Experience – Students gain:

  • Teaching skills
  • Research expertise
  • Administrative experience

4. Career Advancement – Assistantships enhance:

  • Academic CV
  • Employability
  • Networking opportunities

5. Long-Term Funding Potential – Some doctoral programs may offer multi-year funding packages, contingent on performance.

Who Can Apply?

This opportunity is open to a wide range of applicants globally.

Eligible Applicants

  • Domestic (U.S.) students
  • International students from:
    • Africa (including Kenya, Nigeria, Ghana, etc.)
    • Asia
    • Europe
    • Latin America

Graduate assistantships in the U.S. are widely accessible to international applicants, provided they meet admission and visa requirements.

Required Qualifications

To qualify for a graduate assistantship at the University of Tennessee, applicants typically must:

Academic Requirements

  • Be admitted into a graduate degree program (Master’s or PhD)
  • Maintain good academic standing
  • Show satisfactory academic progress

Enrollment Requirements

  • Be registered as a full-time graduate student during the assistantship period

Skills and Competencies

  • Strong academic background
  • Communication and analytical skills
  • Subject-specific expertise

Additional Requirements for International Students

  • Proof of English proficiency (TOEFL/IELTS)
  • Work authorization under student visa regulations

Responsibilities of Graduate Assistants

Graduate assistants are expected to balance academic and work responsibilities effectively. Common expectations include:

  • Working 10–20 hours weekly
  • Meeting departmental performance standards
  • Completing assigned teaching, research, or administrative duties
  • Participating in training or orientation programs

Students must also maintain academic progress to retain funding.

How to Apply for the University of Tennessee Graduate Assistantships

Step 1: Apply for Admission – You must first apply to a graduate program at the University of Tennessee.

Step 2: Check Departmental Opportunities – Assistantships are offered by individual departments, not centrally.

Step 3: Submit Assistantship Application – Some programs require:

  • Separate assistantship application
  • Statement of interest
  • Academic CV

Step 4: Contact Faculty – For research assistantships:

  • Reach out to professors
  • Express research interest alignment

Step 5: Attend Interviews (if required) – Shortlisted candidates may be interviewed before final selection.

Apply here

Important Notes About Availability

  • Assistantships are highly competitive
  • Availability varies by department and funding levels
  • Not all admitted students receive assistantships

Students are encouraged to:

  • Apply early
  • Maintain strong academic records
  • Build relevant experience

Why Choose a Graduate Assistantship in the USA?

Graduate assistantships are among the most strategic ways to fund education in the United States, especially as financial aid options evolve. They provide:

  • Reduced financial burden
  • Practical experience during study
  • Direct engagement with faculty and research

Recent trends also show increased importance of assistantships as alternative funding sources for graduate students.

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: Varies by department and program
  • Most assistantship opportunities are aligned with graduate admissions cycles:
    • Fall intake: December – March
    • Spring intake: September – October

Applicants are strongly advised to apply early alongside their graduate program application to maximize chances.

The University of Tennessee Graduate Assistantship program is more than just a funding opportunity—it is a career-building platform that combines education, employment, and professional growth. For ambitious students worldwide, especially from Africa and other developing regions, it offers a realistic pathway to studying in the United States with reduced financial strain.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Fully Funded Graduate Assistantships at the University of Tennessee 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 at the University of Leeds: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars in Social Sciences
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 hosted by the University of Leeds under the White Rose Doctoral Training Partnership (WRDTP) represent one of the most prestigious and competitive opportunities for early-career researchers in the social sciences. This fellowship is specifically designed to support recent PhD graduates in transforming their doctoral research into impactful publications, strengthening their […]

The post ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 at the University of Leeds: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars in Social Sciences appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 hosted by the University of Leeds under the White Rose Doctoral Training Partnership (WRDTP) represent one of the most prestigious and competitive opportunities for early-career researchers in the social sciences. This fellowship is specifically designed to support recent PhD graduates in transforming their doctoral research into impactful publications, strengthening their academic profile, and advancing toward long-term research careers.

About the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship Programme

The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship scheme is a flagship initiative aimed at researchers in the immediate postdoctoral stage of their careers. The programme is designed to:

  • Help fellows publish findings from their PhD research
  • Develop new research skills and methodologies
  • Build academic and professional networks
  • Strengthen career prospects in academia or policy-related fields

The fellowship focuses primarily on social sciences research, requiring that at least 50% of the proposed project falls within this discipline.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

This fellowship is highly selective and targeted at early-career researchers who meet strict eligibility conditions.

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must:

  • Have completed a PhD at a UK research organisation
  • OR have submitted their thesis and passed their viva with minor corrections by the application deadline
  • Receive their PhD before the fellowship start date

Experience Requirements

  • Must have no more than 12 months of active postdoctoral research experience

Research Requirements

  • Proposed research must:
    • Align with social sciences disciplines
    • Be hosted within an ESRC-accredited Doctoral Training Partnership (DTP)

Eligible Countries and Regions

This is a restricted eligibility fellowship based on institutional affiliation rather than nationality:

  • Open to applicants who completed their PhD at a UK research organisation
  • Applicants from any nationality may apply only if they meet the UK PhD requirement

This means:

  • Applicants from Africa, Asia, Europe, the Americas, and other regions are eligible only if they earned their PhD in the UK

Target Audience: Who Is This Fellowship For?

The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 is ideal for:

  • Recent PhD graduates in social sciences
  • Early-career researchers aiming for academic careers
  • Scholars seeking to publish and expand their doctoral work
  • Researchers interested in policy impact, interdisciplinary work, and global research collaboration

Benefits of the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship

Successful applicants gain access to a wide range of academic and professional benefits:

Financial Support

  • Full funding for the duration of the fellowship
  • Coverage for research-related activities

Career Development

  • Structured training in advanced research methods
  • Mentorship from experienced academics

Research Opportunities

  • Time dedicated to publishing PhD research
  • Opportunity to conduct additional limited research (up to 25%)

Professional Exposure

  • Networking with leading researchers
  • Engagement with policy makers and industry partners

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

The application process is rigorous and typically involves multiple stages:

1. Expression of Interest (EOI)

  • Submit initial proposal, CV, and research outline
  • Internal selection by the university

2. Full Proposal Submission

  • Only shortlisted candidates proceed
  • Submit a detailed research proposal, mentor support letter, and supporting documents

3. Institutional Review

  • Applications are reviewed by the DTP and funding body

4. Final Decision

  • Results typically announced mid-2026
  • Fellowship begins in October 2026

Apply here

Important Requirements to Note

  • Applicants must secure a mentor at the host institution
  • Research proposals must align with approved ESRC pathways
  • Internal university deadlines may be earlier than official deadlines

Why You Should Apply for the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026

This fellowship is more than just funding—it is a career-defining opportunity. It provides a structured pathway from PhD completion to establishing yourself as an independent researcher.

Key advantages include:

  • Prestigious recognition from the Economic and Social Research Council
  • Access to world-class research infrastructure at institutions like the University of Leeds
  • Strong foundation for future academic grants and positions

Key Deadline for ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026

Applicants must pay close attention to the multi-stage application process:

  • Expression of Interest (EOI): Typically between late 2025 and January 2026 (internal deadlines vary by institution)
  • Full Application Deadline:
    • 8th April 2026

Because universities like Leeds conduct internal selection processes, applicants are strongly advised to begin preparations several months in advance.

The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 offer a highly competitive but rewarding pathway for early-career researchers to transition into impactful academic careers. If you recently completed your PhD in the UK and are passionate about advancing research in the social sciences, this fellowship could be your next major step.

For more information click here

For more global opportunities click here

The post ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 at the University of Leeds: Fully Funded Research Opportunity for Early Career Scholars in Social Sciences appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Korea University SK Global Scholarship 2026 (Master’s in AI & Semiconductor Fields)
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The SK Global Scholarship at Korea University is a fully funded master’s degree opportunity for international students seeking to study in one of Asia’s leading academic institutions. Based in Seoul, this scholarship supports students pursuing advanced studies in high-demand fields such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Information Technology (IT), and semiconductor engineering. This program is specifically […]

The post Apply Now: Korea University SK Global Scholarship 2026 (Master’s in AI & Semiconductor Fields) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The SK Global Scholarship at Korea University is a fully funded master’s degree opportunity for international students seeking to study in one of Asia’s leading academic institutions. Based in Seoul, this scholarship supports students pursuing advanced studies in high-demand fields such as Artificial Intelligence (AI), Information Technology (IT), and semiconductor engineering.

This program is specifically designed to develop global talent in cutting-edge technology sectors while fostering international academic collaboration. For students interested in building careers in emerging technologies, this scholarship provides both financial support and access to world-class research and education.

About the Opportunity

The SK Global Scholarship is a competitive program offered through Korea University’s Graduate School. It targets talented international students from selected countries and provides them with the opportunity to pursue a fully funded master’s degree in specialized technical fields.

Students admitted under this scholarship are enrolled in departments related to AI, IT, and semiconductor technologies—areas that are increasingly shaping global economies and innovation systems. The program is structured to provide both theoretical knowledge and practical research experience, ensuring that graduates are well-prepared for careers in academia, industry, or entrepreneurship.

In addition to academic training, students benefit from being part of a diverse international community. Studying in Seoul also provides exposure to South Korea’s rapidly advancing technology sector, home to some of the world’s leading tech companies and innovation hubs.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As global demand for expertise in AI, semiconductors, and advanced technologies continues to grow, access to high-quality education in these fields is more important than ever. However, financial barriers often prevent talented students from pursuing such opportunities.

The SK Global Scholarship plays a critical role in addressing this challenge by:

  • Providing fully funded access to top-tier education
  • Supporting students from diverse backgrounds
  • Promoting international collaboration in science and technology
  • Developing the next generation of global innovators

By studying at Korea University, scholars not only gain technical knowledge but also develop a global perspective, cultural awareness, and professional networks that are essential in today’s interconnected world.

This opportunity is particularly valuable for students who aspire to contribute to technological advancement in their home countries or pursue careers in global industries.

What You’ll Gain

The SK Global Scholarship provides a comprehensive package of financial and academic benefits, allowing students to focus entirely on their studies:

  • Full Tuition Coverage – All tuition fees are covered for the duration of the master’s program (up to four semesters)
  • Monthly Living Stipend – Approximately 1,500,000 KRW per month, helping cover accommodation, food, and daily expenses
  • Round-Trip Airfare – Travel costs are supported, making relocation more accessible
  • Entrance Fee Coverage – Initial admission fees are fully funded
  • Medical Insurance – Health coverage is provided throughout the program
  • Language Support – Opportunities to learn the Korean language and adapt to local culture

Beyond financial support, scholars gain access to modern laboratories, experienced faculty, and industry-relevant research opportunities. These benefits collectively create a strong foundation for long-term career success.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the SK Global Scholarship, applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be an international student from an eligible country
  • Hold (or expect to obtain) a bachelor’s degree before enrollment
  • Demonstrate strong academic performance and potential
  • Meet language proficiency requirements in English or Korean (e.g., IELTS, TOEFL, or equivalent)
  • Apply to a relevant department within Korea University’s Graduate School

Applicants who completed their previous education in English may be exempt from submitting language test scores, depending on the university’s policies.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants must apply through the Korea University Graduate School online application system. The process includes:

  1. Completing the online application form (no application fee required)
  2. Uploading academic transcripts and degree certificates
  3. Submitting proof of language proficiency (if required)
  4. Providing additional supporting documents as requested

Applications are reviewed by departmental committees, and in most cases, interviews are not required unless specifically requested.

Deadline: 31 March, 2026

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Korea University SK Global Scholarship 2026 (Master’s in AI & Semiconductor Fields) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: NIAS Golestan Fellowship 2027–2028 in the Netherlands
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Golestan Fellowship at the Netherlands Institute for Advanced Study is a prestigious, fully funded research opportunity designed for scholars working at the intersection of the medical, biological, and social sciences. Hosted in Amsterdam, this fellowship offers researchers the chance to spend between five to ten months in a highly interdisciplinary academic environment during the […]

The post Apply Now: NIAS Golestan Fellowship 2027–2028 in the Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Golestan Fellowship at the Netherlands Institute for Advanced Study is a prestigious, fully funded research opportunity designed for scholars working at the intersection of the medical, biological, and social sciences. Hosted in Amsterdam, this fellowship offers researchers the chance to spend between five to ten months in a highly interdisciplinary academic environment during the 2027–2028 academic year.

This program is ideal for experienced researchers who want to explore complex global challenges through cross-disciplinary perspectives, combining insights from life sciences with approaches from the humanities and social sciences.

About the Opportunity

The Golestan Fellowship is part of NIAS’s broader mission to support innovative, independent research and foster intellectual exchange across disciplines. The fellowship specifically targets researchers whose work bridges medical or biological sciences with humanities or social sciences, encouraging collaboration and new ways of thinking.

During the residency, fellows will become part of a diverse international cohort of scholars. The program is designed to create space for deep thinking, reflection, and collaboration, away from the usual demands of academic life.

Fellows are expected to actively engage with the NIAS community by sharing their research, participating in discussions, and contributing to interdisciplinary dialogue. The environment emphasizes openness, curiosity, and intellectual risk-taking, making it a unique setting for advancing research ideas.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Many of today’s global challenges—such as public health crises, climate change, and technological transformation—require insights from multiple disciplines. However, academic research is often siloed, limiting collaboration and innovation.

The Golestan Fellowship addresses this by creating a space where researchers can:

  • Break down disciplinary boundaries
  • Develop new frameworks and perspectives
  • Collaborate across fields
  • Produce research that is both scientifically rigorous and socially relevant

By bringing together scholars from different backgrounds, the fellowship promotes holistic approaches to complex problems, helping generate knowledge that can inform policy, practice, and public understanding.

It also provides a rare opportunity for researchers to step away from routine responsibilities and focus on creative, high-impact research in a supportive and stimulating environment.

What You’ll Gain

The Golestan Fellowship offers a range of meaningful benefits that support both research and professional growth:

  • Dedicated Research Time – A five or ten month residency focused entirely on advancing your research project
  • Financial Support – A personal stipend or a grant for affiliated researchers to cover living expenses
  • Access to Facilities – Office space, research infrastructure, and academic resources
  • Subsidized Accommodation – Support with housing arrangements in Amsterdam (where applicable)
  • Global Academic Network – Opportunities to collaborate with international scholars from diverse disciplines
  • Intellectual Exchange – Continuous feedback, discussions, and exposure to new perspectives
  • Career Advancement – Strengthen your research profile and expand interdisciplinary expertise

This fellowship is particularly valuable for researchers looking to deepen their work, explore new directions, and engage with global academic communities.

Eligibility

To be eligible for the Golestan Fellowship, applicants must:

  • Have more than three years of post-PhD research experience
  • Work in the medical or biological sciences, with clear connections to the humanities or social sciences
  • Demonstrate a strong interest in interdisciplinary research
  • Be able to actively participate in the NIAS research community

The fellowship particularly encourages applications from non-Dutch researchers, in line with the vision of the Golestan Foundation.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply for the Golestan Fellowship, candidates must submit their application through the official NIAS website. The application typically includes:

  1. A detailed research proposal
  2. Academic CV and publication record
  3. Supporting documents as required

Applicants should clearly demonstrate how their research bridges disciplines and how they will contribute to the NIAS community.

Deadline: April 13, 2026 (12:00 Noon CET)

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: NIAS Golestan Fellowship 2027–2028 in the Netherlands appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Geography by Practice at the University of Manchester
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The PhD Geography by Practice at University of Manchester is a fully funded doctoral opportunity designed for students who want to combine academic research with creative practice. Open to both home and international applicants, this 3.5-year studentship provides full financial support along with access to a dynamic interdisciplinary research environment in the United Kingdom. This […]

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Geography by Practice at the University of Manchester appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The PhD Geography by Practice at University of Manchester is a fully funded doctoral opportunity designed for students who want to combine academic research with creative practice. Open to both home and international applicants, this 3.5-year studentship provides full financial support along with access to a dynamic interdisciplinary research environment in the United Kingdom.

This program stands out from traditional PhDs by encouraging candidates to explore geography through innovative, creative, and practice-based approaches. It is particularly suited for individuals who want to go beyond conventional academic writing and incorporate creative outputs into their research.

About the Opportunity

The PhD Geography by Practice is a newly developed doctoral program that reimagines how research in geography can be conducted and shared. Rather than focusing solely on written dissertations, this program allows students to integrate creative components into their research projects.

Participants will work on projects that combine geographical scholarship with creative practices such as:

  • Visual arts and exhibitions
  • Film and digital media production
  • Storytelling and creative writing
  • Music and performance
  • Fashion, crafting, and cultural production

Students will be supervised by experts in human geography who specialize in these areas, ensuring strong academic guidance alongside creative exploration. The program is part of a broader effort to expand doctoral training and attract candidates from diverse academic and professional backgrounds.

This unique structure allows students to produce research that is not only academically rigorous but also accessible, engaging, and impactful for wider audiences.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Traditional academic research often limits how knowledge is produced and shared. This program challenges that model by encouraging students to think creatively and explore new ways of communicating ideas.

By combining research with creative practice, students can:

  • Make research more accessible and engaging to wider audiences
  • Explore complex social and cultural issues in innovative ways
  • Contribute to new forms of knowledge production
  • Bridge the gap between academia and creative industries

This approach is increasingly important in today’s world, where interdisciplinary thinking and creative problem-solving are essential for addressing global challenges.

What You’ll Gain

This fully funded PhD offers a wide range of benefits that go beyond financial support:

  • Full Funding for 3.5 Years – Covers tuition fees for both domestic and international students
  • Annual Stipend – Receive approximately £21,805 per year to support living expenses
  • Research Funding – Access an additional £3,000 Research Training Support Grant
  • Creative and Academic Skill Development – Build expertise in both research methodologies and creative practice
  • Interdisciplinary Learning Environment – Engage with a diverse academic community through seminars, workshops, and collaborations
  • Professional Development – Participate in writing retreats, guest lectures, and specialized training sessions
  • Career Opportunities – Prepare for careers in academia, creative industries, research institutions, and beyond

This program is particularly valuable for those looking to build a career that bridges research, creativity, and social impact.

Eligibility

The program is open to a wide range of candidates, particularly those with:

  • A strong academic background in geography or a related discipline
  • Experience or interest in creative practice or cultural production
  • A Master’s or undergraduate degree, especially in interdisciplinary or creative fields
  • A passion for combining research with creative expression
  • The ability to develop an innovative and impactful research proposal

Candidates from creative arts backgrounds or those working in cultural and creative industries are strongly encouraged to apply.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must submit their application through the official University of Manchester funding page. The process typically includes:

  1. Preparing a research proposal that includes a creative component
  2. Submitting academic transcripts and qualifications
  3. Providing supporting documents (such as references and portfolio, if applicable)

Deadline:  01 May 2026

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Fully Funded PhD Geography by Practice at the University of Manchester appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: Open Society Foundations Associate, Program Management (Higher Education) – Multiple Locations
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Associate, Program Management (Higher Education) role at Open Society Foundations is a paid, full-time career opportunity for professionals interested in global development, higher education, and grantmaking. This role is part of the Ideas Workshop under the Higher Education team and is open to candidates based in Bogotá, Mexico City, Rio de Janeiro, or Nairobi. […]

The post Apply Now: Open Society Foundations Associate, Program Management (Higher Education) – Multiple Locations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Associate, Program Management (Higher Education) role at Open Society Foundations is a paid, full-time career opportunity for professionals interested in global development, higher education, and grantmaking. This role is part of the Ideas Workshop under the Higher Education team and is open to candidates based in Bogotá, Mexico City, Rio de Janeiro, or Nairobi.

This is a hybrid position, requiring employees to spend approximately 50% of their time in the office. The role offers a unique chance to work at the intersection of education, philanthropy, and global development while contributing to initiatives that promote academic freedom and equitable access to higher education worldwide.

About the Opportunity

This position plays a central role in supporting the Higher Education team’s grantmaking and program activities. The team focuses on strengthening universities and academic institutions, promoting inclusive education, and supporting students and scholars who face displacement or threats.

As an Associate, Program Management, you will be responsible for managing the full grant lifecycle—from proposal development to reporting and evaluation. You will work closely with partner institutions, ensuring that projects are implemented effectively and aligned with Open Society Foundations’ goals.

Your responsibilities will include coordinating grant processes, reviewing proposals and budgets, managing payments, supporting program design, and collaborating with internal teams across departments. You will also engage directly with partners, helping them navigate grant requirements and strengthen their initiatives.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Higher education plays a critical role in shaping societies, fostering innovation, and advancing democratic values. However, many institutions and students—especially those in vulnerable situations—face barriers to access, funding, and academic freedom.

Through this role, you will contribute to initiatives that:

  • Strengthen universities and academic systems
  • Support displaced and at-risk students and scholars
  • Promote inclusive and equitable education
  • Advance research and critical thinking

By supporting effective grantmaking and partnerships, you help ensure that resources reach institutions and individuals who can create lasting change. This role is essential in maintaining the integrity, efficiency, and impact of global education initiatives.

What You’ll Gain

This role offers significant professional and personal benefits:

  • Competitive Salary & Comprehensive Benefits – Including health insurance, retirement plans, paid leave, and family support
  • Global Career Exposure – Work across multiple regions and collaborate with international partners
  • Specialized Expertise in Grantmaking – Gain hands-on experience managing complex funding processes
  • Professional Development Support – Access to training, learning opportunities, and a generous development allowance
  • Flexible Work Structure – Hybrid work model promoting work-life balance
  • Meaningful Impact – Contribute to advancing academic freedom, equity, and access to education globally
  • Strong Professional Network – Engage with universities, NGOs, policymakers, and global experts

Additionally, the role provides opportunities to participate in events, site visits, and collaborative projects, further enhancing your experience and visibility in the sector.

Eligibility

To be considered for this role, applicants should:

  • Hold a university degree or equivalent experience
  • Have relevant professional experience in NGOs, universities, philanthropy, development, consulting, or public sector work
  • Demonstrate understanding of higher education systems and global trends
  • Have experience in project or program management
  • Possess strong analytical, organizational, and problem-solving skills
  • Be fluent in English (additional languages are an advantage)
  • Be comfortable working across cultures and regions

Experience in grantmaking, working with higher education institutions, or operating across multiple regions is highly desirable.

Application Process and Deadline

To apply, candidates must submit their application through the official Open Society Foundations careers portal. The process typically includes:

  1. Submitting an updated CV/resume
  2. Providing details of relevant experience
  3. Completing any additional application requirements

Shortlisted candidates may be invited for interviews and further assessments.

Deadline: 06 April 2026

Learn more and apply on the offical  Open Society Foundations career portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth
Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Apply Now: Open Society Foundations Associate, Program Management (Higher Education) – Multiple Locations appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply for the Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026: 10-Week AI and Social Sciences Research Program in Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026—also known as the LUNE 3 Fellowship—is a groundbreaking initiative designed to redefine how artificial intelligence (AI) is understood, developed, and governed through African perspectives. This intensive 10-week research fellowship brings together graduate and doctoral researchers to critically engage with AI at the intersection of humanities and social […]

The post Apply for the Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026: 10-Week AI and Social Sciences Research Program in Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026—also known as the LUNE 3 Fellowship—is a groundbreaking initiative designed to redefine how artificial intelligence (AI) is understood, developed, and governed through African perspectives. This intensive 10-week research fellowship brings together graduate and doctoral researchers to critically engage with AI at the intersection of humanities and social sciences.

As global conversations around AI continue to evolve, there is an increasing need for diverse perspectives that reflect local realities, cultural histories, and social contexts. This fellowship addresses that need by equipping African researchers with the intellectual, technical, and collaborative tools required to shape AI systems that are inclusive, ethical, and contextually grounded.

About the Fellowship

The LUNE 3: Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship is specifically tailored for Nigerian graduate and doctoral researchers who are interested in exploring how AI intersects with disciplines such as history, sociology, philosophy, linguistics, political science, and other humanities and social sciences fields.

The program is not just about understanding AI technologies—it is about interrogating them. Fellows are encouraged to question dominant narratives around AI innovation and instead examine how these technologies are shaped by power structures, labor dynamics, and cultural influences.

Participants will develop:

  • Critical analytical skills to assess AI systems and their societal impacts
  • Foundational technical literacy to understand how AI systems function
  • Practice-based research experience grounded in real-world contexts

Ultimately, the fellowship aims to produce scholar-practitioners—individuals who can bridge theory and practice to influence AI development within African institutions and communities.

Fellowship Objectives

The core mission of the fellowship is to reshape AI discourse by centering African epistemologies and lived experiences. Key objectives include:

  • Encouraging interdisciplinary research that integrates AI with humanities and social sciences
  • Supporting locally grounded yet globally relevant scholarship
  • Building a community of researchers engaged in AI critique and development
  • Expanding access to AI research opportunities in low-resource academic settings

The program also seeks to establish a long-term network of researchers who will continue to collaborate and contribute to global AI conversations beyond the fellowship period.

Fellowship Roles and Participation Tracks

The program offers multiple entry points for participants with different expertise and interests. Each role is designed to foster collaboration across disciplines.

1. Fellows

Fellows are individuals with a background in humanities or social sciences who are exploring how AI intersects with their field. They bring domain-specific knowledge and collaborate with technically skilled participants to enrich interdisciplinary research.

2. Navigators

Navigators are participants with technical expertise in AI, computer science, or related fields. They support fellows by providing technical insights while gaining exposure to humanistic and social science perspectives.

This dual structure ensures a mutual exchange of knowledge, where both fellows and navigators benefit from each other’s expertise.

3. Facilitators

Facilitators are experienced researchers or professionals who lead sessions within the program. Each facilitator conducts at least one 90-minute session focused on topics such as:

  • Digital humanities
  • AI systems and applications
  • Ethics and governance of AI

Their role is to deepen participants’ understanding and stimulate critical discussions.

4. Mentors

Mentors provide personalized guidance to participants throughout the fellowship. Each mentor commits 2–4 hours per month to support one or two fellows in developing their research projects.

Mentors are expected to have experience conducting research at global standards and play a crucial role in helping participants produce high-quality outputs.

Fellowship Timeline

The 2026 fellowship follows a structured timeline designed to guide participants from application to final project presentation:

  • April 20, 2026 – Application Deadline
  • Mid to Late May 2026 – Finalists Announced
  • Late May to Early June 2026 – Fellowship Begins
  • Late July to Early August 2026 – Final Presentations and Closing

This timeline ensures a focused and immersive experience within a relatively short duration.

Program Curriculum and Learning Modules

The fellowship curriculum is carefully designed to provide both theoretical grounding and practical research experience.

Module 0: Introduction to AI, Humanities, and Social Sciences

This foundational module introduces participants to the interdisciplinary landscape of AI. Key focus areas include:

  • Understanding AI as a socio-technical system
  • Exploring how power, labor, and culture shape AI technologies
  • Examining dominant narratives versus critical Global South perspectives
  • Mapping individual research interests to AI-related questions

Participants also begin to reflect on their positionality, which is essential for conducting meaningful and context-aware research.

Additional Modules and Workshops

Subsequent modules (Modules 1–3) and additional workshops expand on these themes, offering deeper engagement with specialized topics. While specific details may vary, these sessions are designed to:

  • Enhance interdisciplinary methodologies
  • Introduce practical tools for AI research
  • Encourage collaborative problem-solving

Program Structure

The fellowship is delivered remotely, making it accessible to participants across different locations. To support engagement, the program provides data stipends for all participants.

Key components of the program include:

Weekly Classes

Participants attend 90-minute sessions led by faculty members, covering various AI-related topics.

Targeted Mentoring

Mentors work closely with participants to refine their research ideas and methodologies.

Collaborative Assignments

Participants engage in group work, fostering interdisciplinary collaboration and peer learning.

Exploratory Projects

Each participant develops a research project with guidance from mentors, navigators, and the program team.

Faculty and Mentorship Network

The fellowship boasts a diverse and experienced faculty composed of researchers, academics, and industry professionals working at the forefront of AI and interdisciplinary studies.

The faculty includes experts in:

  • AI governance
  • Computational linguistics
  • Political communication and emerging media
  • Ethnographic research
  • Digital humanities
  • AI policy and law

This broad expertise ensures that participants receive well-rounded guidance and exposure to multiple perspectives.

Research Output and Resources

Participants contribute to a growing body of research that reflects African perspectives on AI. Outputs from previous cohorts, such as the LUNE TWO fellowship, include compilations of ongoing research by fellows and navigators.

These resources serve as valuable references for future participants and contribute to the broader academic and policy discourse on AI.

Sponsorship and Support

The fellowship is supported by a range of organizations committed to advancing AI research and policy in Africa. These partnerships enable the program to provide high-quality training, mentorship, and resources to participants.

Why You Should Apply

The Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship is more than just a training program—it is an opportunity to become part of a transformative movement shaping the future of AI from within Africa.

By participating, you will:

  • Gain interdisciplinary expertise at the intersection of AI and social sciences
  • Work with leading researchers and practitioners
  • Develop a research project with real-world impact
  • Join a network of scholars influencing global AI debates

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply under the role that best matches their background—

Fellow,

Navigator,

Facilitator, or

Mentor.

Each role has specific expectations, but all contribute to a collaborative and intellectually rich environment.

Applicants are encouraged to prepare strong submissions that highlight their interest in AI and its intersection with their field, as well as their commitment to contributing to African-centered AI research.

Conclusion

The Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026 represents a critical step toward diversifying global AI discourse. By centering African perspectives and fostering interdisciplinary collaboration, the program empowers researchers to challenge existing paradigms and build more inclusive, ethical, and context-aware AI systems.

For graduate and doctoral researchers passionate about the intersection of technology, society, and culture, this fellowship offers a unique and impactful opportunity to shape the future of AI.

Discover other similar opportunities here. 

The post Apply for the Shaping AI from African Contexts Fellowship 2026: 10-Week AI and Social Sciences Research Program in Nigeria appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: ESRC LISS Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in the UK (Top Universities, Funded Research Opportunity)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Applications are now open for the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 under the London Interdisciplinary Social Science Doctoral Training Partnership (LISS DTP). This prestigious programme offers early-career researchers the opportunity to strengthen their academic profile, expand professional networks, and further develop research outputs at leading UK institutions including King’s College London, Queen Mary University of London, […]

The post Apply Now: ESRC LISS Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in the UK (Top Universities, Funded Research Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowships 2026 under the London Interdisciplinary Social Science Doctoral Training Partnership (LISS DTP). This prestigious programme offers early-career researchers the opportunity to strengthen their academic profile, expand professional networks, and further develop research outputs at leading UK institutions including King’s College London, Queen Mary University of London, and Imperial College London.

Designed for researchers in the immediate postdoctoral stage, this fellowship focuses on career development rather than funding new research projects. If you are looking to consolidate your PhD and advance your academic career in the social sciences, this opportunity is highly competitive and rewarding.

About the LISS ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship

The LISS DTP is offering five ESRC-funded Postdoctoral Fellowships for the 2026 intake. These fellowships aim to support scholars in enhancing their research portfolios through publications, training, mentorship, and engagement activities.

Rather than funding new research, the programme emphasizes:

  • Strengthening existing PhD research outputs
  • Building academic and non-academic networks
  • Developing professional and transferable skills

Fellows will be hosted at one of the partner institutions and will benefit from a vibrant interdisciplinary research environment.

Fellowship Benefits and Funding

The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship provides substantial support, including:

  • Funding for up to 9 months full-time or 18 months part-time
  • Coverage of the fellow’s salary
  • Up to £7,500 for additional costs, including:
    • Mentorship support
    • Travel and conference participation
    • Training and skills development
    • Public engagement and impact activities

All full-time fellowships must be completed by 30 June 2027.

Mentorship and Collaboration Opportunities

Each fellow is required to work with a mentor based at their host institution. Mentors should:

  • Have expertise in the applicant’s research area
  • Not typically be the applicant’s PhD supervisor

Applicants are encouraged to proactively identify and contact potential mentors before applying. Additionally, collaboration with non-academic partners is strongly supported, offering valuable real-world engagement and impact.

Research Focus and Interdisciplinary Scope

Applications are welcome from:

  • Single-discipline research areas
  • Interdisciplinary projects

However, social sciences must account for at least 50% of the fellowship activities, and proposals must align with one of the LISS DTP’s designated research areas.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the eligibility requirements set by the Economic and Social Research Council. Key criteria include:

  • A completed PhD from a UK research organisation
  • PhD awarded or viva passed (with minor amendments) by 1 June 2026, and fully awarded by 1 October 2026
  • No more than 15 months of active postdoctoral experience by 1 June 2026
  • Only one application to a single Doctoral Training Partnership (DTP)
  • Applicants needing a UK work visa must ensure eligibility

Notably, prior ESRC funding is not required to apply.

Application Process

The application process consists of two stages:

Stage 1: Expression of Interest (EOI)

Applicants must:

  • Review the ESRC call specifications and eligibility criteria
  • Identify and contact a potential mentor
  • Submit an online EOI application
  • Applicants should also complete the online Diversity Monitoring Form 

Deadline: 13 April 2026 at 10:00 AM

Applicants must also complete a Diversity Monitoring Form. Only shortlisted candidates will proceed to the next stage.

Stage 2: Full Proposal

Shortlisted applicants will be invited to submit a full proposal.

Deadline: 1 June 2026 at 23:59

Competition Timeline

  • EOI Deadline: 13 April 2026
  • EOI Results: End of April 2026
  • Full Proposal Deadline: 1 June 2026
  • Interviews: Early July 2026
  • Final Decisions: July 2026
  • Submission to ESRC: By 23 July 2026
  • Fellowship Start Date: 1 October 2026
  • Fellowship End Date: 30 June 2027

Applicant Briefing Session

Prospective applicants can attend a dedicated briefing session to learn more about the fellowship and application process.

  • Date: 27 March 2026
  • Time: 1:00 PM – 2:00 PM

Click Here, for more details on the felowship.

This session is highly recommended for applicants seeking clarity and guidance before submitting their application.

The ESRC Postdoctoral Fellowship at LISS DTP is a valuable opportunity for early-career researchers aiming to build a strong academic foundation in the social sciences. With structured mentorship, financial support, and access to leading UK institutions, fellows are well-positioned to advance their careers and make meaningful contributions to their fields.

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparations early—especially in identifying mentors and refining their research development plans—to maximize their chances of success.

Discover other similar opportunities here. 

The post Apply Now: ESRC LISS Postdoctoral Fellowship 2026 in the UK (Top Universities, Funded Research Opportunity) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
